overview.description: Modal dialog to display content that requires user interaction
overview.purpose: Modal dialog to display content that requires user interaction
whenToUse: When you need to capture user input or display important information.
whenNotToUse: When you need to display a large amount of content or complex layouts.

Vue3.basic:
<mc-modal
  heading="Heading"
  body="value"
  hiddenclose
  fit="small"
  dimension="small"
  padding="none"
  width="value"
  height="value"
  open
  backdropcloseactiondisabled
  escapecloseactiondisabled
  entercloseactiondisabled
  @closed="yourEventHandler(e)"
  @closing="yourEventHandler(e)"
  @opened="yourEventHandler(e)"
  @opening="yourEventHandler(e)"
  @click="yourEventHandler(e)"
  hasheadingslot
  hasbodyslot
  hasfooterslot>
</mc-modal>

VanillaJs.minimal:
<script type="module"
  src="./node_modules/@maersk-global/mds-components-core-modal/index.js"></script>

<mc-modal>
</mc-modal>

<script>
  document.querySelector('mc-modal').addEventListener('closed', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
  document.querySelector('mc-modal').addEventListener('closing', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
  document.querySelector('mc-modal').addEventListener('opened', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
  document.querySelector('mc-modal').addEventListener('opening', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
  document.querySelector('mc-modal').addEventListener('click', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
</script>

props.name: heading
props.description: No heading will be shown, if attribute, or heading slot is not supplied

props.name: body
props.defaultValue: Donec pede justo, fringilla vel, aliquet nec, vulputate eget, arcu. In enim justo, rhoncus ut, imperdiet a, venenatis vitae, justo. Nullam dictum felis eu pede mollis pretium.
props.description: Body should be provided as the default slot i.e.:`<mc-modal>rich text</mc-modal>`

props.name: actions
props.description: There are two named slots available: `primaryAction` & `secondaryAction`. You can pass into those i.e.: confirm or cancel action, like: ```<mc-button slot="primaryAction" dialogaction="ok">OK</mc-button>``` or ```<mc-button slot="secondaryAction" dialogaction="cancel">Cancel</mc-button>``` For more details on usage, please look at code snippet in the example above

props.name: footer
props.description:
There is a slot for additional content to be included in the footer. You can pass into this slot i.e.: instructions or disclaimer, like: ```
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.
```

props.name: dimension
props.description:
On the small screens modal will be shown in full-screen size, regardless of the modal dimension (for small dimension the break point is below `700px`, for medium and large dimension - it is  below `800px`).
      
The specifications of each size preset are as below:
      
- small: `width: 400px`, `height: 225px` 
      
- medium: `width: 800px`, `height: 400px` 
      
- large: `width: 1200px`, `height: 600px`

props.name: padding
props.description: Adjust padding size. For compact look, use `padding="none"`

props.name: width
props.description: Sets the width of the component, otherwise the width of the currently chosen size preset will be used. Remember also to specify the measurement unit i.e. `px`, `%` or `vw`, etc.

props.name: height
props.description: Sets the height of the component, otherwise the height of the currently chosen size preset will be used. Remember also to specify the measurement unit i.e. `px`, `%` or `vh`, etc.

props.name: zindex
props.description: Should only be set if two modal are supposed to be shown on top of each other

props.name: backdropcloseactiondisabled
props.description: This attribute should be added only if clicking on the backdrop (scrim) must not close the modal

props.name: escapecloseactiondisabled
props.description: This attribute should be added only if pressing the `Escape` must not close the modal

props.name: entercloseactiondisabled
props.description: This attribute should be added only if pressing the `Enter` must not close the modal

props.name: autofocus
props.description: Add `autofocus` attiribute to an element inside of modal to set focus on it when modal is opened

props.name: hasheadingslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Set to true when providing content to the heading slot. Not needed for client-side rendering as the slot is detected automatically.

props.name: hasbodyslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Set to true when providing content to the default body slot. Not needed for client-side rendering as the slot is detected automatically.

props.name: hasfooterslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Set to true when providing content to footer/action slots. Not needed for client-side rendering as the slot is detected automatically.

events.name: closed
events.description: The closed event gets dispatched once the modal is closed.

events.name: closing
events.description: The closing event gets dispatched once the modal is closing.

events.name: opened
events.description: The opened event gets dispatched once the modal is opened.

events.name: opening
events.description: The opening event gets dispatched once the modal is opening.

events.name: click
events.description: The click event gets dispatched when clicked on the primaryAction/secondaryAction button within a modal.

slots.description: Use the default slot, when you want to pass body as HTML to the modal. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
Use the primaryAction named slot, when you want to pass confirm action button. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: primaryAction

slots.description: Use the secondaryAction named slot, when you want to pass cancel action button. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: secondaryAction

slots.description: Use the footer named slot, when you want to pass disclaimer or other content in the footer. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: footer
slots.example: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

members.name: backdropcloseactiondisabled
members.description: This attribute should be added only if clicking on the backdrop (scrim) must not close the modal.

members.name: escapecloseactiondisabled
members.description: This attribute should be added only if pressing the `Escape` must not close the modal.

members.name: hiddenclose
members.description: This attribute should be added only if you want to disable default close button.

members.name: padding
members.description: Padding of the content and title in modal.

members.name: zindex
members.description: Should only be set if two modal are supposed to be shown on top of each other.

members.name: width
members.description:
Sets the width of the component, otherwise the width of the currently chosen size preset will be used.
Remember also to specify the measurement unit i.e. `px`, `%` or `vw`, etc.

members.name: height
members.description:
Sets the height of the component, otherwise the height of the currently chosen size preset will be used.
Remember also to specify the measurement unit i.e. `px`, `%` or `vw`, etc.

members.name: open
members.description: Whether the modal should be open.

types.name: McModalAction
types.jsdoc: Indicates the action that was taken when the dialog was closed.

guidelines.title: Modal
guidelines.description: Modals are full-screen takeovers used to direct the user's attention to important information or provide a choice.

best-practice: Modals should be used sparingly because of their interruptive and invasive nature.
Use a modal for quick tasks where the user will not lose the context of the underlying page.
Avoid modals that open suddenly without user interaction.
Avoid modals which are disconnected from what the user is currently doing.
Avoid modals that require additional information outside the modal to be understood.
Avoid multiple modals at once even though it is technically possible
Make sure that the element that triggers the modal is keyboard accessible i.e. it should be possible to move the focus to that element and open the modal by pressing the Enter or Space key.
usage: Modals are used to provide the user with in-context information and functionality relevant to their task at hand. When a modal is shown the user's access to the rest of the page is blocked by a semi-transparent backdrop.
Since modals intentionally disrupt the user's workflow, they should be used sparingly and only when in-page alternatives fail at providing the needed information or functionality.

designPrinciples.title: Spacing
designPrinciples.description: Consistent spacing creates groups, rhythm and good scan-ability.

use-4-pixel-spacing: The base unit for spacing is 4 pixels, subsequent units are multiples of 4 (such as 8, 12, 16, etc.). These units should be used consistently for all paddings, margins, gutters, typography, and layouts to ensure a cohesive appearance and user experience across all Maersk digital products.
exceptions-to-4-pixel-spacing: When creating components, finer control is needed for spacing elements. Three acceptable exceptions here are 2, 6, and 10 pixels.
components: Relate the component elements and still keep them distinct by using space units dervided from the 4-pixel base spacing. These space units will make aligning and using non-core components with core ones easier.
patterns: A recognisable pattern is created when consistent spacing is used to establish a visual rhythm and relationship between the pattern elements.

Pattern spacing needs to be coherent which means not all space units need to be the same. Several spacing units can help establish better visual balance when dealing with differently-sized UI elements in the pattern.
layouts: Visual hierarchy in layouts requires well spaced regions and UI elements. Spacing units can control the information density of any application layout. Use them to provide the right level for the context and users' expectation.
information-density: Space is the element that controls the information density of any interface. Adjusting the space between blocks on information creates a visual rhythm and hierarchy.

High information density can be disorienting and overwhelming if spacing does not allow the eyes to rest and let people process what they see. People may notice the grouped elements but not process each individual item.

We recommend reading the <PageLink path="/design-language/component-fit/">Fit guidelines</PageLink> to help you make an informed decision what spacing units will be most suitable for your layout composition.

relatedGuidelines.title: Accessibility
relatedGuidelines.description: Improving the user experience for everyone.

what-is-accessibility?: Accessibility means that websites, tools and technologies are designed and developed in a way that makes it possible for people with disabilities to use them. Accessibility also benefits people without disabilities for example people with temporary disabilities or situational disabilities.
Accessibility helps people perceive, understand and interact with our digital products and it takes auditory, cognitive, neurological, physical, speech and visual limitations into consideration.

But accessibility is also a mindset and a change in the way we think and talk about digital products. It is a cultural change that cannot be made by one team only. We, therefore, urge all our colleagues as well as top management to get involved and share awareness about this very important topic. We need your support!
why-is-accessibility-important?: One of the main myths about accessibility is that it only concerns a small group of people with severe disabilities. This assumption is far from the reality and according to the World Health Organisation (WHO), more than 1 billion people are living with some level of impairment that affects the way they interact with digital products and access information online.
When our websites, apps and other digital products are designed badly, they create barriers that exclude people and make access to our offerings cumbersome and in some cases not possible at all.
being-inaccessible-is-a-problem: It is an ethical problem when we exclude certain groups of people from our products and services.

It is a legal problem, especially in the US where companies are being sued if they are not accessible, which could have financial consequences in terms of fines as well as affecting the brand negatively.

It is a big business problem - if we choose to exclude a very big group of potential customers because they have a disability.
When we design and build products with accessibility in mind, we not only open up our products and experiences for more people, but our products start to become more human - in the sense that they reflect the diversity of society and become much more flexible and robust to accommodate many different needs and use cases.
